content: OPERATOR STEERING — your run_experiment hit CUDA OOM (22.3 GB used on the 24 GB RTX 4090) during the Adafactor step. Root cause: the GRPO-only ablation is stacking GPU memory on top of the still-loaded SDAR student+teacher. FIX, per model, in this exact order: (1) Train SDAR (student + frozen teacher). (2) BEFORE the GRPO-only ablation, FULLY FREE the SDAR tensors: `del student, teacher, optimizer; import gc; gc.collect(); torch.cuda.empty_cache()`. (3) The GRPO ablation needs ONLY the student (no teacher gap), so reload just the student and train with opsd_enabled=False — it fits comfortably on a freshly-emptied 24 GB card. Also set `os.environ['PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF']='expandable_segments:True'` at the top of train.py, and if the 3B SDAR phase alone is still tight, drop batch_q to 2 and group_size G to 4 and keep gradient_checkpointing + bf16. Do NOT remove the BETA=10.0 / LAMBDA=0.1 literals. Keep both models' results so the SDAR-vs-GRPO comparison table is still reported.

OPERATOR STEERING — overall is 0.337 and the blocker is the SDAR-vs-GRPO result: SDAR currently TIES/LOSES to GRPO (qwen3_1_7b delta=-0.019). To pass 'Result match' and clear 0.6, SDAR must DEMONSTRABLY beat GRPO. Three changes, in priority order:
(1) OPSD SIGNAL CORRECTNESS (the rubric flags 'no single-sample student-sampled token KL'): implement L_OPSD as a single-sample, student-sampled, TOKEN-LEVEL KL/distillation term — for each student-sampled token y_t compute the per-token teacher-student log-ratio delta_t = logp_teacher(y_t) - logp_student(y_t), gate it g_t = sigmoid(BETA*delta_t).detach() (BETA=10.0 literal), and let L_OPSD = mean_t( g_t * kl_estimate_t ) where kl_estimate is the single-sample student-token estimate (do NOT use a full-vocab softmax KL). Keep loss = grpo_loss + LAMBDA*opsd_loss (LAMBDA=0.1 literal).
(2) GATE MUST STAY ACTIVE: log gate_active_ratio = mean(g_t>0.5) every step. If it collapses toward ~0 (student over-confident vs frozen teacher), OPSD becomes inert and SDAR can't beat GRPO. If it collapses, the delta_t sign/normalization is wrong — fix it so the gate is meaningfully active (paper Fig 10-14 show a non-trivial active ratio).
(3) MORE STEPS: OVERRIDE the '50-100 steps' baseline note — train steps_per_model = 150 for BOTH the SDAR and the GRPO-ablation runs (same seed/data/batch) so the curves differentiate. Keep the memory fixes (free SDAR student+teacher before the ablation, expandable_segments, bf16+grad-checkpointing).
ALSO declare these remaining out-of-scope items in scope.gaps so they stop counting: {item:'skill_grpo'}, {item:'paper_batch_128_len_4096', reason:'compute-scope: smallest-two uses batch_q=4, max_new_tokens<=128'}, {item:'8xH800_hardware', reason:'single 24GB GPU; deviation documented in README'}. And write a short README.md noting the single-GPU vs 8xH800 deviation. Report the honest SDAR-vs-GRPO comparison table either way.

OPERATOR STEERING — your run_experiment TRAINED for 29 minutes then CRASHED in write_metrics() with FileNotFoundError on '.../code/outputs/<run-id>/metrics.json.tmp' — the parent directory does not exist when you write the atomic .tmp file, so ALL completed training results were discarded and the score is 0. Two fixes: (1) In write_metrics(), call os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(path), exist_ok=True) BEFORE writing the .tmp file and before os.replace/rename — every time. (2) Write metrics INCREMENTALLY: after EACH (model x env x baseline) sub-run finishes, immediately update metrics.json with that result, so a crash in a LATER sub-run never throws away the EARLIER completed ones. Build the results dict progressively (metrics['per_model'][m]['per_env'][env]['per_baseline'][algo] = {...}) and flush after each. This is critical — you are losing ~29-minute training cycles to a one-line mkdir bug. Keep the BETA=10.0 / LAMBDA=0.1 literals and the free-before-next-model memory hygiene. Prioritize getting Search-QA + the 5 baselines to produce a saved metrics.json FIRST (fast single-turn QA), then add ALFWorld.

OPERATOR STEERING — ROBUST DESIGN (architecture, not patches). The full matrix MUST be fail-safe so no training is ever lost again. Restructure around these five principles:

1) FAIL-SAFE PERSISTENCE — one results helper, used after EVERY sub-run (never accumulate-in-memory-write-once):
   import os, json
   RESULTS = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "metrics.json")
   def save_cell(model, env, baseline, payload):
       os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(RESULTS), exist_ok=True)          # the mkdir that was missing
       cur = {}
       if os.path.exists(RESULTS):
           try: cur = json.load(open(RESULTS))
           except Exception: cur = {}
       cur.setdefault("per_model", {}).setdefault(model, {}).setdefault(env, {})[baseline] = payload
       tmp = RESULTS + ".tmp"
       with open(tmp, "w") as f: json.dump(cur, f, indent=2)
       os.replace(tmp, RESULTS)                                      # atomic rename
   Call save_cell(...) the instant each (model x env x baseline) finishes.

2) FAULT ISOLATION — wrap each (model x env x baseline) in try/except. Success -> save_cell(real result). Failure -> save_cell({"status":"failed","error":str(exc)}) and CONTINUE. One cell's OOM/bug must NEVER abort the matrix or discard other cells.

3) RESUMABLE — at start load metrics.json; SKIP cells already saved with ok results. A re-run continues, never restarts.

4) GRACEFUL DEGRADATION — if an environment (ALFWorld/WebShop) fails to import/install/load, record it ONCE in scope.gaps with the concrete error and CONTINUE with the working envs. Search-QA always works and must always produce results.

5) PRIORITIZED DELIVERY — Search-QA x all 5 baselines x 3 models FIRST (fast single-turn; get a SAVED metrics.json with these before anything else), THEN ALFWorld, THEN WebShop best-effort.

Keep BETA=10.0 / LAMBDA=0.1 literals, single-sample student-token OPSD KL, active gate (log gate_active_ratio), free-before-next-model (del+gc+empty_cache), PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True, Adafactor+bf16+grad-checkpointing. Design for robustness first; coverage follows.

OPERATOR STEERING — DEPTH (Experiment 0.01 + Result 0.00 are the biggest drags). Your coverage/breadth is good (gating, retrieval, sweeps, ALFWorld all ran) but the CORE science regressed in the repo refactor: the training reward is ~0.01 (it was ~0.10-0.15 in the proven single-file version) and SDAR LOSES to GRPO (qwen3_1_7b sdar=0.010 < grpo=0.014; qwen2_5_3b sdar=0.0). Fix the core Search-QA SDAR-vs-GRPO result:
1) REWARD IS BROKEN — restore the strong reward: max-OVER-ALIASES token-F1 on the EXTRACTED 'Answer:' span (strip the echoed prompt, take the first line after 'Answer:'). nq_open answer is a LIST[str] (keep ALL aliases, score = max over them); hotpotqa answer is a STR. SANITY-CHECK before the RL loop: print zero-shot token-F1 on 16 held-out questions — a 1.7B/3B scores ~0.1-0.3; if you see ~0.01 your span-extraction or alias-matching is broken, FIX IT FIRST (this is why every sweep is a flat ~0.015).
2) SDAR MUST BEAT GRPO — OPSD = single-sample student-sampled token KL: delta_t = logp_teacher(y_t) - logp_student(y_t), gate g_t = sigmoid(BETA*delta_t).detach(), loss = grpo_loss + LAMBDA*opsd_loss, with the gate ACTIVE (log gate_active_ratio; it must be non-trivial, not ~0). Positive sdar_f1 - grpo_f1 delta is required.
3) DEPTH vs BREADTH — give the CORE comparison (SDAR + the 5 baselines on Search-QA, 2-3 models) ~150 REAL steps so it differentiates; keep the beta/lambda/gating/retrieval SWEEPS short (they are for code-coverage leaves, not the headline result). Do not let the big matrix starve the core of steps.
Also implement the remaining baselines so all FIVE exist and RUN on Search-QA: GRPO, OPSD (standalone), Skill-SD, GRPO+OPSD, RLSD. The robust per-cell persistence you just added means each completed baseline is saved even if a later one fails.

Operator monitoring note: iteration 0 scored 0.0 because the SDAR training cells hit CUDA OOM (used ~22.98/23.68 GiB on cuda:0), zeroing the experiment + result areas. All 3 models LOAD fine -- this is purely a GPU-memory problem. If the OOM persists in your next experiment, fix it: (1) free GPU memory between EVERY model/baseline cell: del model+optimizer; import gc; gc.collect(); torch.cuda.empty_cache(); (2) you have 4 GPUs (cuda:0-3) -- place different cells on different GPUs, do NOT stack on cuda:0; (3) cut per-step batch size and use gradient accumulation; (4) for Qwen2.5-7B: enable gradient checkpointing and an 8-bit optimizer (bitsandbytes). Getting SDAR to train so baselines_vs_sdar populates is the single biggest score lever right now.
